title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for word definitions, tokenization, boundaries, and offsets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for tested word and token contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Token Invariants
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 3373 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Token Invariants.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Token Invariants
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Token Invariants: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 1372 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to tokenize under a named word policy with nonempty tokens and valid source offsets; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Token Invariants.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Token Invariants, keep Token Invariants connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.
